# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that creates releases and publishes packages. On version tags, build the application, create a GitHub release, and publish to npm/PyPI. Make the release process fully automated so developers can just push tags to trigger releases.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Release Workflow

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - 'v*.*.*'

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Check out code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2

      - name: Set up Node.js (or Python)
        uses: actions/setup-node@v2
        with:
          node-version: '16' # use '3.x' for Python

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install # for Python: pip install -r requirements.txt

      - name: Build application
        run: npm run build # for Python: python setup.py build

  release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build
    steps:
      - name: Check out code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2

      - name: Create Release
        id: create_release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v1
        with:
          tag_name: ${{ github.ref }}
          release_name: Release ${{ github.ref }}
          body: 'Release notes for version ${{ github.ref }}'
          draft: false
          prerelease: false
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Publish to npm (if Node.js)
        run: npm publish
        env:
          NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}

      - name: Publish to PyPI (if Python)
        run: python -m twine upload dist/*
        env:
          TWINE_USERNAME: ${{ secrets.PYPI_USERNAME }}
          TWINE_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.PYPI_PASSWORD }}
